endpoints.name: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn (Battle Styles 133/163)

description: Mirror, sighed but earned. The edge is unusually well-supported on both ends already — Urn's trivia explicitly names the 'co-designed three-piece support suite' and Scroll's trivia names Urn as the recovery complement. Both cards are stamped with the Single Strike emblem on the depicted object itself; iconographically they're twinned still-life ceremonial artifacts (scroll, urn) glowing with internal energy against moody warm-and-dark backgrounds, no figure on either. Mechanical roles are complementary (offensive tool vs energy-recovery engine). Topology tag mirror; flagged as the FIRST member of a potential future stack converging on a Single Strike cycle node (Scroll of Piercing already in corpus = 3rd anchor — Mr. Nodeley threshold met). Deferred per slot-anti-duplicate rule and added to reports/janitor_triage.md future-node candidates list.
citations: [Pokemon.com: Battle Styles Single Strike and Rapid Strike announcement, via WebSearch]
other_candidates_considered: Single Strike mechanic cycle node — Mr. Nodeley threshold met (3 corpus members: Scroll of Scorn, Urn of Vitality, Scroll of Piercing CRE-154) but deferred to janitor_triage future-node list per quad-pass anti-duplicate / weight-management. A future Mr. Nodeley dispatch can build it cleanly.
